germanic_evidence: OHG arawīz/araweiz; MHG ar(e)weiz/arwīz/erbiz; OS erit; MLG erwete; MDu. a(e)rwete/arweete; Du. ert; ON plural ertr; OSw. ært, pl. ærter
non_germanic_comparanda: Gr. ἐρέβινϑος, ὄροβος; Lat. ervum; perhaps OIr. orbaind; Georgian/OCS/Mozarabic forms treated as Greek loans in footnote 5
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Root-noun inflection; Pre-Greek/European agricultural substrate suffix *-ĩd- / *-ind(h)-; comparison with Greek -ινϑος
proposed_source_or_layer: Agricultural substrate / Near Eastern crop Wanderwort mediated into Europe
notes: Central supporting example for unity of Germanic and Pre-Greek agricultural substrate.

germanic_evidence: ON geit, pl. geitr; OE gāt; OHG geiz
non_germanic_comparanda: Lat. haedus; discussion of regional goat words including *dig-, *bhuǵ-, *h₂eiǵ-, *h₂eǵ-, *kapr-, Semitic *gadi̯-, Caucasian comparanda
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Root-noun inflection; semantics tied to goat-keeping/agricultural-pastoral culture; limited areal distribution
proposed_source_or_layer: Old continuum of agricultural and cattle-breeding cultures expanding into Europe; not direct Proto-Semitic in Kroonen’s preferred interpretation
notes: Used against a deeply agricultural Proto-Indo-European model.

article_status: root noun with unclear IE etymology; substrate origin possible
germanic_evidence: ON gnit, pl. gnitr; OE hnitu; OHG niz
non_germanic_comparanda: Ru. gnída; Latv. gnĩda; Gr. κονίς, -ίδος; Alb. thëní; Arm. anic; MIr. sned; Lith. glìnda; Lat. lēns, lendis
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Root-noun inflection; irregular European correspondences; *-id / *-ind alternation linked to agricultural substrate morphology
proposed_source_or_layer: Possible European substrate form approximated as *c~ʕ(o)n-ĩd
notes: Less culturally diagnostic than pea/goat, but formally relevant because of *-ind.

germanic_evidence: ON hnot, pl. hnøtr/hnetr; OE hnutu, pl. hnyte; OHG nuz, pl. nuzzi
non_germanic_comparanda: Lat. nux, gen. -cis; OIr. cnú, gen. cnó; W cneuen, pl. cnau
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Root-noun inflection; incompatible West Indo-European codas; possible final glottal stop or *-ũd(h)- substrate element
notes: Kroonen treats Celtic-Germanic similarity as suspect absent a Germano-Celtic proto-language.

gloss: lady, woman of high standing; goddess/matron
article_status: root noun with potentially non-Indo-European origin
germanic_evidence: OE idis; OHG itis; OS idis; ON dís; cultural parallel dís Skjǫldunga / idis Scildinga
non_germanic_comparanda: No secure extra-Germanic cognates accepted; Indo-European etymologies via *h₁edh-ḗs or *h₂eidh- are rejected as semantically/formally weak
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Root-noun inflection; internal Germanic *edis ~ *dīsi- alternation compared speculatively with a-prefixation and stress-related reduction
notes: Important because linguistic and mythological evidence are used together.

article_status: probably originally consonant/root noun; non-Indo-European migratory term
germanic_evidence: OHG wisunt/-ant; ON visundr; OE we(o)send; OHG plural wisunti; proper name Wirunt; Gothic Οὐισανδος in Procopius
non_germanic_comparanda: OPruss. wissambs’; Lith. stum̃bras; Latv. sumbrs/sūbrs; OCS zǫbrь; Ru. iz’ubr’; Caucasian comparanda including Ossetic/Adygh/Georgian dombaj and Abkhaz a-dəwp-èy
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Consonant/root-noun behavior; irregular Balto-Slavic/Germanic forms; possible *wi- prefix; non-IE coda variation *-ombr / *-ond
proposed_source_or_layer: Extinct North-European language / migratory term entering Germanic and Balto-Slavic separately
notes: Kroonen dates adoption before Proto-Germanic breakup but after Proto-Balto-Slavic split, roughly mid-first millennium BC.

germanic_evidence: ON styrja; OHG sturio; OE styrgia
non_germanic_comparanda: Ru. osëtr; SCr. jèsetra; Lith. erškẽtras; OPruss. esketres
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Alternation of initial vowel in Balto-Slavic vs zero in Germanic; discussed under a-prefixation
notes: Presented as candidate despite Schrijver’s reduction rule not applying straightforwardly.

germanic_evidence: None direct; used to interpret PGm. *arwīt- suffix morphology
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Combines a-prefixation with *-ĩd(h)- / -ινϑ- type suffix; root alternation *a-ggl-ĩd(h)- / *gegl-ĩd(h)-
notes: Used to argue that a-prefixation and the ind(h)-suffix belong to the same substrate complex.

article_status: borrowed/contact word, not inherited PIE in Kroonen’s treatment
germanic_evidence: ON þjórr; Dutch dialect duur; Gothic/OE/OHG steer forms under *steura-
non_germanic_comparanda: Lat. taurus; Gr. tauros; Lith. taũras; OCS tur; Alb. ter; OIr. tarb; Proto-Semitic *ṭawr; Etr. thevrumines
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Irregular *t/*st/*þ correspondences; northern *e vs Latin *a; possible fronting of *a in source/intermediary language
notes: Used as possible support for fronted *a > *e in pre-Germanic source material.

article_status: possible external Wanderwort/contact item discussed as analogue
non_germanic_comparanda: Hitt. ḫasterza; Gr. ἀστήρ; Lat. stella; Skt. tāraḥ / stŕ̥bhiḥ; Akk. ištar; Hebr. ᶜaštōreṭ; SArab. ᶜṭtr
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Irregular Sanskrit forms; possible substitution of Semitic ayin and *ṭt cluster by h₂ and *(s)t
proposed_source_or_layer: Proto-Semitic or external source entering IE dialect continuum
notes: Not a Germanic root noun; included because it supports Kroonen’s broader argument about Wanderwort phonological irregularity.

non_germanic_comparanda: Lat. aper; OCS veprь; Latv. vepris
substrate_marker_or_morphology: Initial *w vs zero possibly compared with *dzombr ~ *widzombr in bison word
notes: Included as comparandum for the proposed *wi- prefix in *wisund-.
